name: fused non-gated xIELU FFN (Apertus)

op.signature: down_proj( xIELU( up_proj(x); alpha_p, alpha_n, beta, eps ) )

preconditions: x, w_up, w_down are contiguous row-major
alpha_p, alpha_n are 1-element tensors on x's device
alpha_p, alpha_n are the raw LOG-SPACE params (checkpoint form): effective scales are softplus(alpha_p) and beta + softplus(alpha_n)
beta = 0.5, eps = -1e-6 (Apertus defaults); passed as python scalars, not tensors

numerics.notes: Apertus (swiss-ai/Apertus-8B-Instruct-2509) uses a NON-GATED FFN: up_proj -> xIELU -> down_proj, with no gate_proj (unlike fused_ffn@1.0.0's SwiGLU). The xIELU activation (arXiv:2411.13010, issue #80) is parametric (learned alpha_p/alpha_n log-space + fixed beta/eps) and piecewise on sign(x); it is evaluated in fp32 then cast to the input dtype, matching xielu@1.0.0. The reference reuses the standalone xielu reference (xkernels.ops.activation.reference:xielu) so reference and triton card share ONE activation precision path -> bit-identical activation, and only the projections (identical torch.matmul) plus the triton-vs-torch xIELU fp32-ULP differ. The down-projection sums a mixed-sign intermediate over the contracted dim N (Apertus N=21504, larger than fused_ffn's typical N), so the same catastrophic-cancellation analysis as issue #82 applies: a last-ULP activation difference is amplified to ~1e-5..1e-4 at moderate-magnitude outputs, so fp32 rtol is 1e-4 (not 1e-5) — the cancellation floor, not a looseness. bf16/fp16 bars match the fused_ffn / xielu family (1.6e-2 / 1e-3).
